hero.eyebrow: Department of Mechanical Engineering · Inha University
hero.title: Flow Noise Control Laboratory
hero.subtitle: We study the physics of flow-induced noise and design turbomachinery that runs efficiently and quietly.
hero.imageCaption: CFD-based aerodynamic noise simulation of a fan

about.title: About the Lab
about.lead: The Flow Noise Control Laboratory (FNC Lab) works at the intersection of turbomachinery, aeroacoustics, and fluid power — from fundamental flow physics to production-ready machine design.
paragraphs: Our work spans multi-fidelity turbomachinery optimization, appliance design, aeroacoustics and noise control, construction-machinery hydraulics and control, micro-dust transport, and wind energy.
We pair high-fidelity CFD with computational aeroacoustics in a multiphysics framework to resolve unsteady flow structures and trace the mechanisms that generate noise.
The lab is equipped with a climate wind tunnel, an anechoic chamber, hydraulic test rigs, flow-diagnostic instrumentation, and in-house design software that supports a broad R&D portfolio.

principles.title: Principles to product
principles.body: Good design starts from physical understanding. We work across theory, simulation, and experiment.

principles.title: Quiet is efficient
principles.body: Reducing noise means reducing losses. We refuse to separate silence from performance.

principles.title: A lab that grows together
principles.body: Peers, industry mentors, and a collaborative network — we learn and build together.

research.title: Multi-Fidelity Turbomachinery Optimization
research.body: Design and optimization of axial and centrifugal fans using multi-fidelity CFD and machine-learning surrogates, built on our in-house iDesignFan framework to maximize aerodynamic efficiency while minimizing noise.

links.title: ServUair — Fan Model Selection
links.desc: Enter diameter, S.P.L, and volume flow rate to find a matching fan model.

videos.title: “Aerodynamic & Aeroacoustic Optimization of Fan Impellers via Multi-Fidelity CFD and ML Design Algorithms” — Invited Lecture, KFMA 2025 Winter Conference

research.title: Appliance Design
research.body: Integrated design of fans, filters, and flow paths for home appliances such as air purifiers — co-optimizing airflow, clean-air delivery, and acoustic performance with our iDesignAir toolset.

videos.title: Micro Dust Research Center: Smart Range-Hood Technology — Overview & Comparative Demo

links.title: AeroNet · Life Style Innovation Range Hood
links.desc: A next-generation range hood applying convergence technology — over 20% higher exhaust capacity, with CO₂ and particulate-matter sensors enabling AI auto-operation for strong extraction while cooking and natural ventilation at rest.

research.title: Aeroacoustics & Noise Control
research.body: Prediction, measurement, and control of flow-induced noise — combining hybrid CFD/CAA, anechoic sound-power testing, and phased-array beamforming to localize and reduce noise sources.

research.title: Construction-Machinery Hydraulics & Control
research.body: Modeling and control of hydraulic systems for construction machinery — from main control valve (MCV) dynamics to multi-machine swarm coordination — for responsive, stable, and efficient operation.

links.title: Software-Defined Machine Workforce Development Program for Construction Equipment
links.desc: Program overview for the Software-Defined Machine (SDM) workforce project, covering swarm control for construction machinery (KIAT · KOCETI).

links.title: 3D-Based Construction Equipment Design & Analysis Workforce Development Program
links.desc: Program overview for the 3D-based construction-equipment design & analysis workforce project (KIAT · KOCEMA).

research.title: Micro-dust Study
research.body: Simulation and mitigation of particulate-matter transport across large spaces such as ports and airports, including AIR WINDOW barriers and vegetative filtration systems.

links.title: Inha University Micro Dust Research Center
links.desc: Inha University's Micro Dust Research Center — inventory assessment of particulate matter, social problem-solving through dialogue with Incheon citizens and experts, and academic micro-dust solutions.

research.title: Wind Energy & Wind Tunnel
research.body: Wind-energy research and integrated aerodynamic, thermal, and acoustic testing in a digital climate wind tunnel with real-time control of temperature, humidity, and wind speed.

links.title: AeroNet · Wind Power (AN Windjet)
links.desc: AN Windjet, an innovative vertical-axis wind turbine — high-efficiency aerodynamic design with low-noise (< 40 dBA) operation and reduced bird-strike risk, suited to distributed community wind farms.

facilities.title: Anechoic Chamber · Acoustics
facilities.body: Anechoic chamber and spherical microphone array for sound-power measurement and beamforming source localization.

facilities.title: Digital Wind Tunnel
facilities.body: A climate wind-tunnel environment with real-time monitoring of temperature, humidity, and wind speed.

facilities.title: Acoustic Camera · Beamforming
facilities.body: Beamforming acoustic-camera measurement that visualizes noise-source locations in indoor spaces.

facilities.title: PIV · Flow Visualization
facilities.body: PIV and smoke-visualization equipment observing flow structures with a laser sheet and seeding particles.

professor.name: Seungbae Lee
professor.title: Professor / Principal Investigator
professor.bio: A researcher in flow-induced noise, turbomachinery, CFD, and multiphysics 3D analysis. He earned his Ph.D. at UCLA on Large-Eddy-Simulation-based aeroacoustics, led the Flow Team at Samsung Electronics, and has been a professor in Mechanical Engineering at Inha University since 1996. He currently directs the Fine Dust Research Center at Inha University.
professor.thesis: Subgrid-scale Modeling in Large-Eddy Simulation and Its Application to Aerosound

career.role: Director, Fine Dust Research Center, Inha University
Professor, Construction Machinery Engineering, Inha University
Professor, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering, Inha University (former Chair)
Director of R&D, Office of Research, Inha University
Visiting Professor, Von Karman Institute / Caltech
Flow Team Leader (Senior Researcher), Samsung Electronics Living System Lab

recruit.title: We are looking for students to work with us.
recruit.lead: Students interested in fluids, acoustics, or mechanical design — we welcome your inquiries.

items.title: M.S. / Ph.D. Students
items.body: Interest in fluid mechanics, CFD, and acoustics matters more than perfect grades. We look for persistence and curiosity.

items.title: Undergraduate Researchers
items.body: Short-term research during the semester or breaks — starting with CFD basics and lab assistance.

items.title: Industry-Linked Programs
items.body: On-the-job Ph.D. tracks and contract research with industry are welcome.

recruit.cta: Send a short intro and your topic of interest by email.
